activities_projects.title: Case Study Analysis of Pakistan under Musharraf
activities_projects.objective: Understand why some governments are not democratic
steps: Read the case study about Pakistan under General Musharraf
Identify why the government was considered undemocratic
Write a short explanation using democratic features
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students analyse real examples of non-democratic governance
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of analysis and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Features of Democracy Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand the four key features of democracy
steps: List the four features of democracy from the chapter
Provide a brief explanation for each feature
Add examples from real-world governments
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students identify and explain democratic principles
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and clarity of chart (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Election System Comparison
steps: Study examples of elections in China and Mexico
Identify problems with these elections
Write a comparison with democratic elections
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the concept of free and fair elections

activities_projects.title: One Person One Vote Survey
steps: Ask classmates why equal voting rights are important
Collect at least five responses
Summarize the importance of equal voting rights
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and summary of responses (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rights of Citizens Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Recognize the importance of citizen rights
steps: List basic democratic rights mentioned in the chapter
Discuss why these rights are necessary
Write examples of how rights protect citizens
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of rights in democracy

activities_projects.title: Group Debate on Democracy
steps: Divide the class into two groups
One group argues for democracy
The other discusses its limitations
Present conclusions
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students evaluate arguments for and against democracy

activities_projects.title: Democracy vs Dictatorship Table
activities_projects.objective: Differentiate between democratic and dictatorial governments
steps: Create a table with two columns
Write features of democracy and dictatorship
Include examples of countries

activities_projects.title: Role Play on Democratic Decision Making
steps: Create a scenario where a class decision must be made
Allow students to discuss options
Take a vote and decide collectively

activities_projects.title: Importance of Free Media Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of media in democracy
steps: Collect news articles about government policies
Identify how media reports on political issues
Write a short note on the role of media
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand media’s role in democracy

activities_projects.title: Poster on Democratic Values
steps: Create a poster representing democracy
Include concepts like equality, freedom, justice
Present the poster in class
